records.ety: [L. recreatus, p. p. of recreate to create anew, to refresh; pref. re- re- + creare to create. See Create.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Recreated (-`td); p. pr. & vb. n. Recreating.]

defs: To give fresh life to; to reanimate; to revive; especially, to refresh after wearying toil or anxiety; to relieve; to cheer; to divert; to amuse; to gratify.
quotes: Painters, when they work on white grounds, place before them colors mixed with blue and green, to recreate their eyes, white wearying . . . the sight more than any.
St. John, who recreated himself with sporting with a tame partridge.
These ripe fruits recreate the nostrils with their aromatic scent.
